\begin{document}
\begin{center}
{\Huge\bfseries 般若波羅蜜多心經}\\[0.5em]
{\LARGE Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya}\\[0.3em]
{\Large Heart Sūtra}\\[1.5em]
{\large Chinese Critical Edition}\\[0.5em]
{\normalsize Based on T251 (Xuanzang, ca.\ 649--656 CE)}\\[2em]
\end{center}

\hrule
\vspace{1em}

\section*{Witnesses}

\begin{description}[leftmargin=2cm, labelwidth=1.8cm]
\item[\wit{T251}] 般若波羅蜜多心經 (ca. 649-656 CE).
\item[\wit{T250}] 摩訶般若波羅蜜大明呪經.
\item[\wit{Fangshan}] 房山心經 (13 March 661 CE).
\item[\wit{T256}] 唐梵飜對字音般若波羅蜜多心經 (Tang dynasty (uncertain, possibly 8th-9th century)).
\item[\wit{T257}] 佛說聖佛母般若波羅蜜多經 (1005 CE).
\end{description}
\vspace{1em}
\newpage

\section*{Text}

\subsection*{Section I: Opening}
\linenum{1} 觀自在菩薩，\footnote{\lemma{自在菩薩}] \reading{觀世音菩薩，行深般若波羅蜜時，照見五陰空，度一切苦厄。} \wit{T250}. 觀世音 (Guānshìyīn) vs T251 觀自在 (Guānzìzài) - different transliteration of Avalokiteśvara}\\
\pinyin{Guānzìzài púsà}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{2} 行深般若波羅蜜多時，\\
\pinyin{xíng shēn bōrě bōluómìduō shí}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{3} 照見五蘊皆空，\\
\pinyin{zhàojiàn wǔyùn jiē kōng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{4} 度一切苦厄。\\
\pinyin{dù yīqiè kǔ è}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{When Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva was practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā, he illuminated the five aggregates and saw that they are all empty, thus transcending all suffering.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{When Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva was practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā, he illuminated the five aggregates and saw that they are all empty, thus transcending all suffering.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section II: Form and Emptiness}
\linenum{5} 舍利子，\footnote{\lemma{子}] \reading{舍利弗，非色異空，非空異色，色即是空，空即是色。受想行識亦如…} \wit{T250}. 非色異空 (fēi sè yì kōng) vs T251 色不異空 (sè bù yì kōng) - 'it is not that form differs from emptiness' vs 'form does not differ from emptiness'}\\
\pinyin{Shèlìzǐ}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{6} 色不異空，\\
\pinyin{sè bù yì kōng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{7} 空不異色；\\
\pinyin{kōng bù yì sè}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{8} 色即是空，\\
\pinyin{sè jí shì kōng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{9} 空即是色。\\
\pinyin{kōng jí shì sè}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{10} 受想行識亦復如是。\\
\pinyin{Shòu xiǎng xíng shí yì fù rúshì}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Śāriputra, form is not different from emptiness, emptiness is not different from form; form is emptiness, emptiness is form. Feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness are also like this.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Śāriputra, form is not different from emptiness, emptiness is not different from form; form is emptiness, emptiness is form. Feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness are also like this.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section III: Characteristics}
\linenum{11} 舍利子，\footnote{\lemma{子}] \reading{舍利弗，是諸法空相，不生不滅，不垢不淨，不增不減。} \wit{T250}. Essentially identical to T251:3}\\
\pinyin{Shèlìzǐ}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{12} 是諸法空相，\\
\pinyin{shì zhūfǎ kōng xiāng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{13} 不生不滅，\\
\pinyin{bù shēng bù miè}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{14} 不垢不淨，\\
\pinyin{bù gòu bù jìng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{15} 不增不減。\\
\pinyin{bù zēng bù jiǎn}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Śāriputra, the emptiness characteristic of all dharmas is: not arising, not ceasing; not defiled, not pure; not increasing, not decreasing.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Śāriputra, the emptiness characteristic of all dharmas is: not arising, not ceasing; not defiled, not pure; not increasing, not decreasing.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section IV: Negations: Skandhas}
\linenum{16} 是故空中，\footnote{\lemma{是故空中}] \reading{舍利子。是故空中。無色。無受想行識。無眼耳鼻舌身意。無色聲香…} \wit{T257}. }\\
\pinyin{Shìgù kōng zhōng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{17} 無色，\\
\pinyin{wú sè}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{18} 無受想行識，\\
\pinyin{wú shòu xiǎng xíng shí}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Therefore, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, perception, formation, or consciousness;}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Therefore, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, perception, formation, or consciousness;}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section V: Negations: Āyatanas}
\linenum{19} 無眼耳鼻舌身意，\footnote{\lemma{無眼耳鼻舌身}] \reading{无眼耳鼻舌身意，无色聲香味觸法，} \wit{Fangshan}. identical (orthographic variant)}\\
\pinyin{wú yǎn ěr bí shé shēn yì}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{20} 無色聲香味觸法，\\
\pinyin{wú sè shēng xiāng wèi chù fǎ}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{no eyes, ears, nose, tongue, body, or mind; no forms, sounds, smells, tastes, tangibles, or mental objects;}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{no eyes, ears, nose, tongue, body, or mind; no forms, sounds, smells, tastes, tangibles, or mental objects;}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section VI: Negations: Dhātus}
\linenum{21} 無眼界，\footnote{\lemma{無眼界}] \reading{无眼界，乃至无意識界，} \wit{Fangshan}. identical (orthographic variant)}\\
\pinyin{wú yǎn jiè}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{22} 乃至無意識界。\\
\pinyin{nǎizhì wú yìshí jiè}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{no eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{no eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section VII: Negations: Pratītyasamutpāda}
\linenum{23} 無無明，\footnote{\lemma{無無明}] \reading{无无明，亦无无明盡，乃至无老死，亦无老死盡，} \wit{Fangshan}. identical (orthographic variant)}\\
\pinyin{Wú wúmíng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{24} 亦無無明盡，\footnote{\lemma{無無明}] \reading{无无明，亦无无明盡，乃至无老死，亦无老死盡，} \wit{Fangshan}. identical (orthographic variant)}\\
\pinyin{yì wú wúmíng jìn}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{25} 乃至無老死，\\
\pinyin{nǎizhì wú lǎosǐ}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{26} 亦無老死盡。\\
\pinyin{yì wú lǎosǐ jìn}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{No ignorance and no ending of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, and no ending of old age and death.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{No ignorance and no ending of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, and no ending of old age and death.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section VIII: Negations: Noble Truths}
\linenum{27} 無苦集滅道，\footnote{\lemma{無苦集滅道}] \reading{无苦集滅道，无智亦无得，以无所得故。} \wit{Fangshan}. identical (orthographic variant)}\\
\pinyin{Wú kǔ jí miè dào}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{28} 無智亦無得，\\
\pinyin{wú zhì yì wú dé}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{29} 以無所得故。\\
\pinyin{yǐ wú suǒ dé gù}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{No suffering, origination, cessation, or path; no wisdom and no attainment, because there is nothing to attain.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{No suffering, origination, cessation, or path; no wisdom and no attainment, because there is nothing to attain.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section IX: Bodhisattva Result}
\linenum{30} 菩提薩埵，\footnote{\lemma{提薩埵}] \reading{菩薩依般若波羅蜜故，心無罣礙，無罣礙故，無有恐怖，離一切顛倒…} \wit{T250}. 菩薩 (púsà) vs T251 菩提薩埵 (pútísàduǒ) - abbreviated vs full transliteration of bodhisattva}\footnote{\lemma{菩提薩埵}] \reading{舍利子。由是無得故。菩薩摩訶薩。依般若波羅蜜多住。心無障礙。…} \wit{T257}. }\\
\pinyin{Pútísàduǒ}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{31} 依般若波羅蜜多故。\\
\pinyin{yī bōrě bōluómìduō gù}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{32} 心無罣礙，\\
\pinyin{Xīn wú guà'ài}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{33} 無罣礙故。\\
\pinyin{wú guà'ài gù}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{34} 無有恐怖，\\
\pinyin{Wú yǒu kǒngbù}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{35} 遠離顚倒夢想，\\
\pinyin{yuǎnlí diāndǎo mèngxiǎng}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{36} 究竟涅槃。\\
\pinyin{jiūjìng nièpán}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Because bodhisattvas rely on prajñāpāramitā, their minds are without obstruction. Without obstruction, they have no fear, are far removed from inverted views and dreams, and attain ultimate nirvāṇa.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Because bodhisattvas rely on prajñāpāramitā, their minds are without obstruction. Without obstruction, they have no fear, are far removed from inverted views and dreams, and attain ultimate nirvāṇa.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section X: Buddha Result}
\linenum{37} 三世諸佛，\\
\pinyin{Sānshì zhū fó}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{38} 依般若波羅蜜多故，\footnote{\lemma{多故}] \reading{三世諸佛，依般若波羅蜜故，得阿耨多羅三藐三菩提。} \wit{T250}. 般若波羅蜜 vs T251 般若波羅蜜多 - T250 lacks 多 (duō)}\\
\pinyin{yī bōrě bōluómìduō gù}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{39} 得阿耨多羅三藐三菩提。\\
\pinyin{dé ānòuduōluó sānmiǎo sānpútí}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{All buddhas of the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, attain anuttarā-samyak-saṃbodhi.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{All buddhas of the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, attain anuttarā-samyak-saṃbodhi.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section XI: Mantra Praise}
\linenum{40} 故知般若波羅蜜多，\footnote{\lemma{多}] \reading{故知般若波羅蜜，是大明呪，是無上明呪，是無等等明呪，能除一切…} \wit{T250}. THREE epithets (大明呪、無上明呪、無等等明呪) vs T251 FOUR epithets (大神咒、大明咒、無上咒、無等等咒)}\footnote{\lemma{故知般若波羅}] \reading{是故應知般若波羅蜜多。是大神咒。是大明咒。是無上咒。是無等等…} \wit{T257}. }\\
\pinyin{Gùzhī bōrě bōluómìduō}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{41} 是大神咒，\\
\pinyin{shì dà shén zhòu}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{42} 是大明咒，\\
\pinyin{shì dà míng zhòu}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{43} 是無上咒，\\
\pinyin{shì wúshàng zhòu}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{44} 是無等等咒，\\
\pinyin{shì wú děngděng zhòu}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{45} 能除一切苦，\\
\pinyin{néng chú yīqiè kǔ}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{46} 眞實不虛故。\\
\pinyin{zhēnshí bù xū gù}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Therefore know that prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great illumination, the supreme mantra, the unequalled mantra, which can remove all suffering; it is true and not false.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Therefore know that prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great illumination, the supreme mantra, the unequalled mantra, which can remove all suffering; it is true and not false.}

\vspace{1em}

\subsection*{Section XII: Mantra}
\linenum{47} 說般若波羅蜜多咒，\footnote{\lemma{說般若波羅蜜}] \reading{故說般若波羅蜜多呪，即說呪曰：揭諦揭諦，波羅揭諦，波羅僧揭諦…} \wit{Fangshan}. The Fangshan reading 薩婆訶 (sàpóhē) is a more accurate transliteration of Sanskrit svāhā than T251's 僧莎訶 (sēng suōhē), which appears to be corrupt.}\footnote{\lemma{說般若波羅蜜}] \reading{故說般若波羅蜜呪，即說呪曰：竭帝竭帝，波羅竭帝，波羅僧竭帝，…} \wit{T250}. 竭帝 (jiédì) vs T251 揭帝 (jiēdì) - different character for 'gate'}\footnote{\lemma{說般若波羅蜜}] \reading{故說般若波羅蜜多咒。即說咒曰。唵 誐帝誐帝 波囉誐帝 波囉僧…} \wit{T257}. }\\
\pinyin{Shuō bōrě bōluómìduō zhòu}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{48} 卽說咒曰：\\
\pinyin{jí shuō zhòu yuē: Jiēdì jiēdì}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{49} 揭帝揭帝，\\
\pinyin{bōluó jiēdì}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{50} 波羅揭帝，\\
\pinyin{bōluó sēng jiēdì}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{51} 波羅僧揭帝，\\
\pinyin{pútí sēng suōhē}\\[0.3em]
\linenum{52} 菩提僧莎訶。\\
\pinyin{Shuō bōrě bōluómìduō zhòu, jí shuō zhòu yuē: Jiēdì jiēdì, bōluó jiēdì, bōluó sēng jiēdì, pútí sēng suōhē.}\\[0.3em]

\vspace{0.5em}
\gloss{Therefore proclaim the prajñāpāramitā mantra, proclaim the mantra that says: Gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.}

\vspace{0.3em}
\fluent{Therefore proclaim the prajñāpāramitā mantra, proclaim the mantra that says: Gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.}

\vspace{1em}

\end{document}
